body.title: Aider control-arm first run: harness gap CONFIRMED, model lever priced (qwen 2.15x faster)
body.text:
# Aider builder first run — decisive result (2026-07-06)

Ran aider (external dep, isolated ~/.aider-venv, subprocess-only, v0.86.3.dev) headless against Hex ollama ($0) on a deterministic capability task (implement TTLCache so 4 failing pytests go green), isolated sandbox (own git, no UU remote). Runner: devlab/claudecode/aider_smoke.py (re-runnable, model-parameterized).

## Results
| Arm | Result | Wall |
|---|---|---|
| DS loop + devstral-small-2:24b (prior baseline, 07-04) | 0 edits (budget-exhaustion) | ~34min then HALT |
| aider + devstral-small-2:24b@Hex | GREEN 4/4 | 35.6s |
| aider + qwen3-coder:30b@Hex | GREEN 4/4 | 16.6s |

## What this proves (2 confounds resolved empirically)
1. HARNESS GAP is real and LARGE. Same model (devstral-24b), same Hex inference, same implement-till-green task shape DS produced 0 edits on -> aider makes it GREEN in 35.6s. The wall was the LOOP, not the 24B model. This is the harness-gap datapoint Fable's 2x2 was designed to produce; the aider arms are done.
2. MODEL LEVER priced. qwen3-coder:30b (MoE ~3B active) is 2.15x FASTER than dense devstral-24b on the same box AND completes the task. Confirms Akien's iteration-velocity thesis: qwen is the builder model. Latency win compounds across every future run.

## Consequences for the plan
- Aider builder is VIABLE NOW on our $0 Hex inference. Fastest path to a swarm builder = aider + qwen3-coder, wrapped as a device.
- Validates Akien's ordering hard: aider builder first (known quantity), THEN level DS to MATCH aider (DS-leveling target becomes 'be as good as aider's loop', not invented from scratch).
- 2x2 status: aider x {devstral,qwen} = both GREEN (here); DS x devstral = 0-edits (baseline); DS x qwen = the remaining interesting cell (T-qwen-vs-devstral-architect-probe covers the DS side).
- Caveat (honest): TTLCache is a 2-file TOY. Proves the mechanism, NOT that aider holds on a real multi-file orientation task (where DS died in read-wandering). Next rung: aider on a harder multi-module task before calling the builder 'one we're happy with'.
- Not in io_corpus: aider called Hex ollama directly via litellm (external), so these bytes are NOT corpus-captured. Stage-2 OpenAI-compatible proxy endpoint (separate ticket) would fix that.

## Reproduce
python3 devlab/claudecode/aider_smoke.py --model qwen3-coder:30b   # RED sandbox -> aider -> GREEN
